titleStmt.title: SII 16.112: original edition by H. K. Narasimhaswami and G. S. Gai
No. 112. (A.R. No. 607 of 1907.) NANDALŪR, RAJAMPET TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a slab set up near the inner gōpura of the Saumyanāthasvāmin temple.

The inscription is dated Śaka 1456, Manmatha, Pushya śu. 15. If the Śaka year quoted is a mistake for 1457, the details correspond to 1536 A.D., January 8, (Saturday). The Makara-saṅkramaṇa referred to in the record occured on December 28, twelve days earlier than the date of the record.
It records the restoration by royal order of the gift village Āṇḍupūru to god Tiruveṅgaḷayyanātha by Rāmābhaṭlayya, on the representation of Tāḷapāka Tirumalayyaṅgāru.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
ṁ śubhamastu . Avighnamastu .
svastiśrī jayābhyudaya śālivā
hana śakhavaruṣaṁbulu 1456 A
gunēṁṭi manmatha saṁvvatsara puṣya
śu 15 lu śrīman®mahārājādhirāja
rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa
śrīvīra Accutarāyamahārāya
lu vidyānagara siṁhvāsanamaṁddu pṛ
thivīsrāmrājyaṁ śēyucuṁḍḍaṁgā
nu nelaṁdalūri cokkanāthaperumā
ḷaku pūrvaṁ śāsanasthamayina grāmaṁ
Āṁḍupūru rājikāna kilamayivu
ṁḍaṁgānu yī grāmaṁ tāḷapā
ka tirumalayyaṁgāru Accutarā
ya mahārāyalakuṁ jeppi Ā grā
maṁ cokkanāthaperumāḷaku rā
yalacāta yippiṁcci rāmabaṭla
yyavārikini rāyalacēta Āna
tiṁ yippiṁcci Āṁḍupūriṁgrāma
ṁ tathātithi makarasaṁkramāṇa pu
ṇyakālamaṁddu Accutarāya
mahārāyalukuṁ buṁṇyaṁgānu
rāmābaṭlayyavāru tiruveṁgaḷanātha
ni sanitini sarvamānyaṁgrāmaṁgānu
dhāravōśi Āceṁdrā¨rkastāyigānu peru
māḷaku sama¨rppiṁcciri yī dha¨rmānaku
yaṁvaru tappinānu vāraṇāśilōnu gō
brāhmala vadhiṁccina pāpāna tama talliṁda
ṁḍrula vadhiṁccina pāpānaṁ bōvuvāru
svadattā dviguṇaṁ puṁṇyaṁ paradattānupā
lanaṁ . paradattāpahārēṇa svadattaṁ niṣphalaṁ
bhavētu . svadattaṁ vvā paradattaṁ vvā yō ha
rēta vasuṁddarā . ṣaṣṭhi¨rvvaruṣasahasrā
ṇi viṣṭhāyāṁ jāyatē krimi ḥ maṁgaḷa
mahāśrīśrīṁjēyunū .
Digital edition of SII 16.112 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
